diary:
Started the day on the patio with coffee, watching the haze roll in from those BC fires. The heat was already creeping up by mid-morning—91 degrees by afternoon, they said. Work was steady; had some good conversations with kids who needed to talk things through. Molly Moon's was a lifesaver around lunch—ducked in for ice cream and air conditioning, watched the Capitol Hill crowd shuffle past the windows.

After work I swung by Elliott Bay Books, lost myself in the stacks for a few minutes. There's something grounding about that place. Drove home to freshen up, then headed over to Gas Works Park for the concert. The whole neighborhood turned out—blankets, coolers, that perfect golden light before sunset. Ran into some folks from school, actually felt good to be around people. The smoke from up north gave everything this soft, dreamlike quality. Drove home tired but settled.
thought_process: The heat and smoke made me want to seek out cool, quiet spaces early on—coffee on the patio was my way of easing into the day despite the air quality. Work felt purposeful; kids open up more when it's hot and everyone's a little slower. I grabbed ice cream partly for relief, partly just to interrupt the routine. The bookstore stop was selfish maybe, but I needed those few minutes alone before the evening. When I heard about the free concert, I almost skipped it—fatigue was settling in—but something pushed me to go. Being 51 and surrounded by neighbors and live music felt like the right antidote to a long day. The smoke actually made it prettier somehow, less harsh. I'm glad I didn't stay home.
world_event_prompt: Hot, clear Wednesday hitting 91F by mid-afternoon. Free outdoor concert at Gas Works Park from 6pm draws big crowds. Moderate air quality from distant BC wildfire smoke.
